name: Traditional Residential - Rustic
description: Single family and 2-3 unit buildings. Select other uses like schools, parks, places of worship and daycares, etc. The lot size, lot width, and building setbacks decrease as you go down the list.
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-unit building

name: Suburban Residential - Consistent 1
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2 unit building

name: Suburban Residential - Consistent 2
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2 unit building

name: Suburban Residential - Consistent 3
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2 unit building

name: Traditional Residential - Consistent 1
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2 unit building

name: Traditional Residential - Consistent 2
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2 unit building

name: Traditional Residential - Consistent 3
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2 unit building

name: Traditional Residential - Consistent 4
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3 unit building

name: Suburban Residential - Varied 1
description: Ranges from single family to townhomes and small multifamily buildings, and select other uses like schools, parks, places of worship and daycares, etc. Mixed-use buildings are also allowed but are limited to uses like a daycare, church or gym.
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 3-4 unit townhome, 4-8 unit multifamily
residential_conditional: >4 unit townhome, Mixed-use building

name: Traditional Residential - Varied 1
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 3-4 unit townhome, 4 unit multifamily
residential_conditional: Mixed-use building

name: Suburban Residential - Varied 2
description: Ranges from single family to large multifamily buildings, and select other uses like schools, parks, places of worship and daycares, etc. Mixed-use buildings are also allowed but are limited to uses like a daycare, church or gym.
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 3-8 unit townhome, 4-24 unit multifamily
residential_conditional: >8 unit townhome, >24 unit multifamily, Mixed-use building

name: Traditional Residential - Varied 2
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 3-4 unit townhome, 4-12 unit multifamily
residential_conditional: >4 unit townhome, 13-60 unit multifamily, Mixed-use building

name: Traditional Residential - Urban 1
description: Ranges from single family to large multifamily buildings, and select other uses like schools, parks, places of worship and daycares, etc. These residential districts have the highest maximum allowed density. Mixed-use buildings are also allowed but are limited to uses like a daycare, church or gym.
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 3-8 unit townhome, 4-24 unit multifamily
residential_conditional: >8 unit townhome, >24 unit multifamily, Mixed-use building

name: Traditional Residential - Urban 2
residential_permitted: 3 unit building, 3-8 unit townhome, 4-36 unit multifamily
residential_conditional: Single family building, 2 unit building, >8 unit townhome, >36 unit multifamily

name: Traditional Residential - Planned
description: Large areas that provide a mix of housing types, and select other uses like schools, parks, places of worship and daycares, etc. Sites that are more than 10 acres require approval of a Master Plan by Common Council.
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 5-8 unit townhome, >60 unit multifamily

name: Limited Mixed-Use
description: Small-scale mixed-use development within or adjacent to residential areas. Building size is limited to 5,000 square feet.
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 1 to 2-unit mixed-use buildings

name: Neighborhood Mixed-Use
description: Medium-scale mixed-use areas
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 4-12 unit multifamily, 1-24 unit mixed-use building
residential_conditional: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 3 or > unit townhome, 13-24 unit multifamily, >24 unit mixed-use building

name: Traditional Shopping Street
description: Mixed-use street corridors
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 5-24 unit multifamily, 1-48 unit mixed-use building
residential_conditional: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 3 or > unit townhome, 4 unit multifamily, >24 unit multifamily, >48 unit mixed-use building

name: Commercial Corridor - Transitional
description: Historically auto-oriented commercial corridors that should become mixed-use corridors
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 5-36 unit multifamily, 1-60 unit mixed-use building
residential_conditional: Single family building, 2-3 unit building, 3 or > unit townhome, 4 unit multifamily, >36 unit multifamily, >60 unit mixed-use building

name: Commercial Center
description: Historically auto-oriented, large-format retail and office sites that should become mixed-use centers
residential_permitted: Live-work unit
residential_conditional: 3 or > unit townhome, 4 or > multifamily, 1 or > unit mixed use building

name: Regional Mixed Use
description: High-intensity mixed-use centers with a variety of multifamily housing options and commercial activities that serve the needs of the region. These areas typically include large-scale sites with multistory buildings and should be the most intensively developed areas in the city outside of the downtown.
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 3 or > unit townhome, 4 or > multifamily, 1 or > unit mixed use building

name: Mixed-Use Center
description: Used to redevelop or establish new compact, mixed-use centers. This zoning district requires approval of a Master Plan.
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 3 or > unit townhome, 4-36 unit multifamily, 1-60 unit mixed-use building
residential_conditional: >36 unit multifamily, >60 unit mixed-use building

name: Tiny House Village
description: More than 3 tiny houses on one property. Can include a community building for residents. Limited commercial and manufacturing uses are allowed.
residential_permitted: Tiny houses

name: Downtown Residential 1
description: Areas in Downtown with smaller residential buildings. There is a maximum building width of 60 feet. Mixed-use buildings are also allowed but are limited to uses like a daycare or church.
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3-unit building, 3-8 unit townhome, 4 unit multifamily
residential_conditional: >4 unit multifamily, Mixed-use building
max_density: 3,000 sq ft minimum lot size for all uses

name: Downtown Residential 2
description: Areas in Downtown with smaller residential buildings. Limited commercial uses are permitted in mixed-use buildings.
residential_permitted: Single family building, 2-3-unit building, 3-8 unit townhome, 4-8 unit multifamily
residential_conditional: >8 unit multifamily, Mixed-use building
max_density: 3,000 sq ft minimum lot size for all uses

name: Urban Office Residential
description: Areas in Downtown that have a combination of office uses and multifamily residential uses in close proximity to residential only areas.
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 3 or > unit townhome, 4-24 unit multifamily, 1-24 unit mixed-use building
residential_conditional: >24 unit multifamily, >24 unit mixed-use building

name: Urban Mixed Use
description: High-density mixed-use areas
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 3 or > unit townhome, 4 or > unit multifamily, Mixed-use building

name: Downtown Core
description: High-density mixed-use areas that includes the Capitol Square, the State Street corridor, and surrounding properties. Residential uses are appropriate in some locations or in combination with other uses. This district is intended to allow intensive development with high-quality architecture and urban design.
residential_permitted: Live-work unit, 4 or > unit multifamily, Mixed-use building

name: Suburban Employment
description: Employment areas that can include limited industrial uses. Residential requires conditional use approval. The minimum lot size is larger than the TE District.
residential_conditional: Live-work unit, >8 unit townhome, Multifamily building, Mixed-use building

name: Traditional Employment
description: Employment areas that can include limited industrial uses. Residential requires conditional use approval. The minimum lot size is smaller than the SE District.
residential_conditional: Live-work unit, >8 unit townhome, Multifamily building, Mixed-use building

name: Employment Campus
description: Large areas that function as a campus, with a mix of employment and specialized manufacturing. Residential requires conditional use approval. This zoning district requires approval of a Master Plan by Common Council.
residential_conditional: Live-work unit, >8 unit townhome, Multifamily building, Mixed-use building

name: Suburban Employment Center
description: Used for existing office and business parks. Properties are no longer rezoned to this zoning district. This zoning district requires approval of a Master Plan by Common Council.
residential_conditional: Mixed-use building

name: Industrial Limited
description: Light industrial uses, offices, warehousing and limited commercial uses

name: Industrial General
description: Heavy industrial uses, offices, warehousing and limited commercial uses

name: Agriculture
description: Cultivation and other agricultural uses. Farm houses are allowed.
residential_permitted: Single family building

name: Urban Agriculture
description: Smaller-scale agricultural uses within developed areas

name: Conservancy
description: Natural areas with limited recreational uses. Areas can also be used for stormwater.

name: Parks and Recreation
description: Active outdoor and indoor recreation uses, institutional uses

name: Airport
description: For Dane County Regional Airport uses

name: Nonmetallic Mineral Extraction
description: Nonmetallic mining operations

name: Mission Camp
description: A campground for temporary or permanent tents and portable shelters operated by a non-profit or government entity. Intended for people experiencing homelessness.
residential_permitted: Government operated
residential_conditional: Non-profit operated

name: Campus Institutional
description: For educational and medical institutions. Requires approval of a Master Plan by Common Council when a property is rezoned to CI or a new property is created and zoned CI.
residential_permitted: Dormitories, Student and faculty housing

name: Planned Development
description: Property-specific district where allowable uses, height, density, building placement, and design are defined only for that site. Planned Development Districts require approval by Common Council.
residential_permitted: Determined by Planned Development District
residential_conditional: Determined by Planned Development District

name: Planned Mobile Home Park
description: Property-specific district for mobile home parks. There are different requirements for new and existing mobile home parks. Planned Development Districts require approval by Common Council.
residential_permitted: Mobile homes
